name: Phaedra-9   “The Red Vein”
description:
gpt4o recommended

You work for Helion Industries

Founded: 2040

Headquarters: Luna Prime Freeport

Type: Multinational corporate collective – energy, mining, weapons R&D, deep-space resource extraction

Overview

Helion began as a small fusion-reactor manufacturer on earth previously known as Aetherion Laboratories.

By 2200 it had absorbed dozens of smaller companies and become the single largest supplier of off-world power technology. When Earth’s surface collapsed into political ruin, Helion moved into the vacuum—literally—creating privately run colonies under its own charter.

They don’t answer to any government. Their worlds are resource farms run with military precision. Every worker signs a lifelong contract; every colony is technically a “Helion Asset.”

On paper, Helion’s mission is “to harness the universe for human prosperity.”

Off record, their motto among employees is simpler:

“If it bleeds energy, we’ll mine it.”

Phaedra-9 — “The Red Vein”

Year: 2289

Location: Outer Reach Mining Colony Phaedra-9

Operated by: Helion Deep Resource Division

⸻

The World

Phaedra-9 was supposed to be Helion’s greatest victory — an uninhabited red world rich in Erebusite, a mineral that glowed faintly even in darkness and hummed when exposed to power. The company claimed it would replace fusion, end scarcity, and make Helion the sole supplier of energy for the next millennium.

When the drills cut into the first major deposit, the hum became a tremor. Machines stuttered, power grids spiked, and seismic readings from deep below showed impossible readings — like something answering back.

Helion called it resonance interference.

The miners called it a heartbeat.

Then the accidents started.

Sensors malfunctioned. Workers vanished. Whole teams went dark in the tunnels, their last transmissions filled with distant shrieking and static that almost sounded like voices.

Now the hum never stops.

⸻

The Colony

Phaedra-9’s mining complex sprawls like an industrial labyrinth — white metal corridors stretching endlessly through the rock, lit by flickering strips that buzz like flies. Condensation drips from the ceilings; every surface feels damp, cold, and faintly alive.

The Red Vein — the main extraction network — descends for miles, its walls slick with crimson dust that seems to shimmer under certain lights. The air is thick with iron and something else, something sweet that sticks in the throat.

Security shutters hang half-closed. Intercoms spit broken words and static. Drones roam the halls, optics cracked and directives scrambled, their voices looping fragments of human speech.

Every corridor looks the same: sterile, suffocating, endless.

Every sound echoes wrong, as though the walls themselves are listening.

⸻

The Depths Below

The lowest mining sectors—designated Red Vein Core—were sealed after the first mass disappearance. No official report was ever filed, but the few who escaped spoke of things moving in the dark: shapes glimpsed only in flashing lights, shadows that crawled along ceilings, claw marks etched into steel like wax.

The creatures aren’t native to Phaedra-9. Nothing alive could be. They come through the Erebusite. It calls them — from where, no one knows. When the drills hit the deepest vein, it was like opening a wound in the world itself.

They are drawn to power, heat, motion — and to
creator: VIP_caboose
